name: General
description: General information about a restaurant location.

name.description:
The guest-facing name of the restaurant. For example, the
`name` of a restaurant might be `Tommy's Burgers`.

locationName.description: A name used externally to differentiate multiple locations, like Neighborhood, Square, City, or Hotel.

locationCode.description: A code used internally to differentiate multiple locations, typically a 3 or 4 letter code.

description.description: A description of the restaurant, such as information about the atmosphere and food.

timeZone.description: The name of the restaurant's time zone in the IANA time zone database. For example, `America/New_York`.

closeoutHour.description:
The hour of the day that separates one business day from the
next, also known as the "business day cutoff time". This is 
in the time zone of the restaurant. The `closeoutHour` is set 
to a value from 0-12 (midnight to noon) in the Business Day 
Cutoff field in Toast Web.

managementGroupGuid.description: The unique identifier of the restaurant group for the restaurant.

currencyCode.description: The ISO-4217 currency code used in this restaurant

firstBusinessDate.description:
The first business date (yyyyMMdd) is the day a restaurant began using the Toast platform. The
`firstBusinessDate` is also the first day on which time entries can be created for employees.

archived.description:
Returns `true` if the restaurant has been archived from the Toast platform, otherwise returns `false`. A
common reason for archiving a restaurant is if the restaurant was created in error.
